title: Agent swarms and the new model economics · Cursor
text: Cursor reports the new swarm reached 80% of the held-out SQLite suite in four hours with Grok 4.5 where the old swarm spiraled, and every new configuration later passed 100% of the suite. The Opus 4.8 planner plus Composer 2.5 workers mix cost $1,339 versus $10,565 for GPT-5.5 everywhere, with the worker fleet costing only $411.

title: Scaling long-running autonomous coding · Cursor
text: Cursor's earlier swarm post explains the planner-worker split that replaced flat coordination. Hundreds of workers ran concurrently, the browser experiment wrote over 1 million lines across 1,000 files, and the hierarchy solved lock bottlenecks and risk-averse churning.

title: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals | Anthropic
text: Anthropic found a 6 point swing on Terminal-Bench 2.0 between strict and uncapped resource setups, with infra error rates falling from 5.8% at 1x to 2.1% at 3x headroom and 0.5% uncapped. Even SWE-bench moved by 1.54 points when RAM increased from 1x to 5x.

title: Writing effective tools for agents — with agents | Anthropic
text: Anthropic recommends standing tools up in a real agent client, generating held-out tasks from real workflows, and measuring tool runtime, token use, errors, and redundant calls. The post shows a concise response shrinking from 206 tokens to 72 and says tool-description refinements pushed Claude Sonnet 3.5 to state-of-the-art SWE-bench Verified performance.

title: Building effective agents | Anthropic
text: Anthropic argues that successful agent systems stay simple, use evaluator-optimizer loops when feedback is clear, and invest heavily in agent-computer interfaces. While building its coding agent, the team says it spent more time optimizing tools than the overall prompt and fixed filepath mistakes by requiring absolute paths.

title: What we've learned building cloud agents · Cursor
text: Cursor says moving cloud agents from a work-stealing loop to Temporal took reliability from one 9 to past two 9s. The current loop handles more than 50 million actions per day across more than 7 million workflows, and more than 40% of internal PRs already come from cloud agents.

title: Development environments for your cloud agents · Cursor
text: Cursor's environment post says agents are only as capable as the environments they run in. Multi-repo environments, Dockerfile-based environment definitions, scoped build secrets, and layer caching that makes rebuilds 70% faster support the durability and verification story for background agents.

title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition | OpenAI
text: OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol scoring 80 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, 2.8 points above Claude Fable 5 while using 54% fewer output tokens, taking 57% less time, and costing about one-third less. Terra and Luna preserve much of that coding quality at lower price points, and Lovable says GPT-5.6 cut steps by 25%, tool calls by 35-48%, and stuck runs by 15%.

title: A scorecard for the AI age | OpenAI
text: OpenAI argues that model routing should optimize useful intelligence per dollar rather than raw token price. The company recommends tracking cost per successful task, retries, human review, and accepted outcomes, and uses GPT-5.6 as an example of a more capable model producing better results at lower total cost.
